Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.10` is an alpha release focused on source-located type
explanations, stricter type-system boundaries, and compiler workflow hardening.

Contract v0 stable-core and the Contract v1 standard/resource slices remain
closed. This release carries type obligations from inference through
specialization so general subtype failures can point back to their actual
source causes in both the CLI and browser playground. It also makes ordinary
casts and generic role implementations fail closed, lands the first production
Yumark static core, and adds an ordinary pull-request / main-branch CI gate.

## Highlights

- General `UnsatisfiedSubtype` failures now retain bounded source provenance
  across inference, poly, and specialization. Tuple-arity, nested and generic
  tuple, record-field, and polymorphic-variant mismatches keep their existing
  primary message and add related information at the source expression that
  created the incompatible obligation. The same structured cause reaches the
  CLI and wasm playground.
- Made required ordinary value casts cardinality-explicit and fail-closed:
  no visible declaration produces a missing-implicit-cast diagnostic, one
  declaration selects that cast, and multiple matching declarations produce a
  deterministic ambiguity diagnostic with candidate locations instead of
  choosing by declaration order.
- Added a persistent, bounded constraint-derivation account so source-backed
  cast errors can be distinguished from internal solver replay consequences.
  Missing-cast diagnostics now identify the inferred argument and required
  callee boundary; for same-session unannotated parameters constrained by an
  `if` condition, they also point to the body use that required `bool`.
- Enforced the approved explicit-associated-type conformance boundary for
  generic role implementations. Binder-aware value/effect surfaces,
  prerequisites, recursive bounds, and visible cast policy are checked without
  narrowing an invalid generic implementation until it appears to fit, and
  mismatches identify the affected method.
- Expanded source-located diagnostics for not-callable expressions, missing
  record fields, non-record selections, unhandled effects, host-capability
  failures, and role-method failures. CLI carets now account for display width;
  LSP diagnostics retain cross-file attribution and safer hover ranges; the
  playground renders the same structured labels, hints, related ranges, and
  source frames.
- `yulang check` now returns failure when analysis produces error diagnostics,
  no longer repeats lowering errors, and prints check statistics only when
  explicitly requested.
- Added the first production Yumark static core: grouped block commands, a
  typed algebra-passing value model, the full static vocabulary, and standard
  HTML / Markdown algebras. Documentation comments are associated with their
  declarations, preserve structural blank lines and line continuations, and
  can be rendered lazily by a resident language-server hover worker.
- Improved playground execution semantics around effects and top-level
  nondeterminism. Side-effecting runs no longer reuse an unsafe result cache,
  the evidence VM has an explicit `--print-nth` route, and the playground
  presents effect output plus only the final root value by default.
- Hardened compiled-unit reuse with canonical non-empty boundary interfaces,
  structural fingerprints shared by typed and runtime surfaces, malformed
  payload rejection, and conservative fallback from unsafe std-prefix
  boundaries. Role-heavy inference also avoids repeated candidate work through
  subscription-aware owner scheduling and smaller hot-path allocations.
- Closed the Contract v1 ambient line-editing parity slice for
  `&doc.lines.each` update chains across the native host, a source mock, and
  clean unsupported-host capability denial.
- Added the first ordinary repository CI gate. Pull requests and pushes to
  `main` now run `cargo fmt --check` plus the focused poly, specialize, parser,
  infer, and yulang test suites. Tag-triggered releases also run the hardened
  performance gate before GitHub Release publication.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.10
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.10
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- Source-cause explanations are intentionally additive and
  availability-aware. General subtype provenance is cold-build / same-build,
  and callee-body cast provenance is same-session; imported or fully
  cache-loaded code keeps the primary diagnostic but may omit deeper related
  ranges.
- The canonical cache boundary is active, but full cached/cold semantic
  equivalence and the broader candidate-lifecycle fallback policy remain
  active validation tracks. Unsafe std-prefix boundaries continue to fall back
  conservatively.
- Yumark command / inline-expression injection, source spans on algebra
  operations, and a dedicated playground preview are not part of this slice.
- Publishing the GitHub Release does not automatically deploy the hosted
  playground or documentation site. Those web surfaces may temporarily lag the
  wasm/compiler revision in this release.
- The new ordinary CI gate covers formatting and the selected compiler/CLI
  suites. A warning-free clippy gate and automated playground deployment remain
  follow-up work.
- Server resources remain at the alpha startup slice: HTTP adapters, natural
  termination, timeouts, backpressure, cancellation surface, and the connect
  side remain future work.
